turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: I've just been feeling really terrible for the past two weeks - my cough won't go away and it's started producing this yellowish-green stuff. It's also been really hard to breathe and I feel like I'm getting winded doing even small things, which is really frustrating because I'm an elementary school teacher and I need to be able to keep up with the kids.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: It's just so exhausting and embarrassing too - I feel like I'm letting my students down by not being able to teach them properly, and it's hard for me to keep up with lesson plans when I'm feeling like this. And on top of that, my partner is also a bit under the weather, so I'm worried we might have passed something back and forth to each other...

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: Yeah... I've also had a bit of a low-grade fever, usually in the evenings, it's not super high but it's there. And sometimes my body feels really hot and then I'll wake up drenched in sweat - it's pretty annoying, to be honest!

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: I actually have been noticing that I get really hot and sweaty at night, sometimes so much so that my pajamas are soaked through. It's not just a little sweat, it's like my whole body is drenched. And yeah, I've also felt a bit lightheaded when I'm standing up or getting out of bed, but it usually goes away after I sit back down for a minute.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: The cough and wheezing are mainly on my right side, and it feels like my whole chest is tight. When I'm taking deep breaths or coughing, it feels like a sharp pain on that same side, but it's not constant, it comes and goes.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: Not really, just my chest area. But to be honest, I've been feeling really fatigued and weak, even after resting or taking naps. It's like I don't have any energy anymore, which is totally not me - I'm usually a pretty active person.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: I haven't noticed any swelling, but my appetite has definitely been off lately. I've just been feeling really full and don't feel like eating much of anything. It's weird because I usually love food!

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: No, actually my stomach feels pretty normal, no nausea or discomfort there. But I did want to mention that since all of this started, I've been taking over-the-counter cough medicine with dextromethorphan, but it hasn't really helped with my symptoms.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: My energy's been really low, like I said, and my appetite's definitely off - I've just not been feeling hungry at all lately. It's weird because I usually love food and look forward to mealtimes, but now even thinking about eating makes me feel tired. And to be honest, it's hard for me to focus on anything other than how bad I'm feeling, which is making it tough to keep up with work and daily tasks.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: I've actually been having a low-grade fever, usually around 100.8 degrees Fahrenheit, and it's usually worse in the evenings. Like I said earlier, sometimes my body feels really hot and then I'll wake up drenched in sweat. It's pretty annoying, to be honest!

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I have seasonal allergies, which cause my allergic rhinitis, and I take over-the-counter cetirizine when it flares up. And... (pausing slightly) ...I'm also allergic to penicillin - taking it makes me break out in a rash. Other than that, no major medical conditions, just the usual check-ups and vaccinations as an adult.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I've actually been pretty good about sticking to my cetirizine when my allergies act up. But with this cough and fever, it's been harder for me to remember to take my usual allergy meds - I guess I'm just too focused on feeling better from this other stuff.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: To be honest, since all of this started, my appetite has been off and I've just not felt like eating much of anything. Even when I am hungry, it's hard for me to focus on food because everything else feels so overwhelming. Usually, I'm a pretty healthy eater, trying to get in lots of fruits and veggies and whole grains, but lately, I've just been surviving on whatever is easy and convenient, which isn't great for me at all.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: I try to stay active, but it's been really hard lately. Even just getting out of bed feels like a chore, let alone going for a walk or doing some yoga. I've been trying to do some gentle stretches in bed when I can, but that's about it. My partner has been super supportive and helps me with errands and stuff, which is great because I don't have the energy to do them myself right now.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma, so I've grown up seeing her deal with that. And my maternal grandmother actually passed away from lung cancer a few years ago. But other than that, I don't think there's anything else significant in our family history. (pausing slightly) I guess it's also worth mentioning that several students in my class have been sick with upper respiratory infections recently, so maybe we just got hit with something like that?

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient:
It's definitely crossed my mind, especially since I've been feeling so unwell and my partner is under the weather too. But I try not to dwell on it and focus on getting better instead.

Oh, and one more thing - I did take a physical exam about a year ago and everything was normal at that time.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I have a glass of wine on occasion, maybe 1-2 glasses on weekends when my partner and I are socializing. As for coping with stress, I've always been pretty good at managing it - I just make sure to get enough sleep, exercise regularly (when I can), and try to take time for myself when things get overwhelming. But lately, it's been really hard to find the energy or motivation to do any of those things...

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: I don't smoke and I've never used any recreational drugs. To be honest, it's been really tough to even think about anything other than my symptoms lately, so it's not like I've had time to worry about those things... (pausing slightly) But yeah, just to confirm, no smoking or substance use for me.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient:
I think we've covered a lot already, but if I'm being completely honest, it's hard for me to talk about how much this is affecting my work and my self-image. As an elementary school teacher, I take pride in being able to show up and do my job, and right now, that's just not possible. It feels like I'm failing somehow, even though deep down I know that's not true. But it's hard to shake that feeling when you're as invested in your work as I am.

And one more thing - I've been trying to take care of my cat while my partner is under the weather, but he's getting a bit anxious and stressed because he misses his routine too. Does that sound silly?

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so... actually, one thing has been on my mind lately - my family's history of lung issues. With my grandmother passing away from lung cancer and my mom having asthma, it's hard not to worry about whether there might be something more serious going on with me right now. Does that sound irrational?
